results.title: Residential Movers Near Me: Best Days to Move in 2026
results.content:
## Outstanding service from start to finish!

> We are so grateful to Murphy Moving for a flawless move. We have used Murphy three other times, and we will happily use them again for any future moves. Len and Lisa were super helpful on the front end in providing a quote, scheduling the move, giving us suggestions, and answering all of our questions. They checked in throughout the process to make sure all was going well. Our movers, Marcelo and Fillipe, were amazing. They encountered more than a few challenges with multiple stairs, and lots of tight turns in narrow hallways. Their attitudes were very pleasant throughout and all of our belongings were handled with much care. They did a great job!
>
> - Jamie

Somers, NY

## We valued our trusting relationship with Murphy Moving and Storage [...] ### Demand Drives Availability and Scheduling

Moving companies have a limited number of crews, trucks, and daily time slots. When demand spikes, those resources fill quickly.

Weekends, holidays, and end-of-month dates book up fastest because they align with common schedules and lease timelines. As demand rises, available options quickly become limited.

Weekday moves, especially Monday through Thursday, spread demand more evenly, making scheduling easier and more flexible.

### Peak Periods Concentrate Moving Activity

Certain times consistently see higher moving volume:

 Late spring through early fall
 Weekends
 The first and last week of the month

### Flexible Timing Expands Your Options

The more flexible your moving date, the more options you will have. [...] > We were very fortunate to find Murphy Moving and Storage for our complicated relocation that also required storage. Geraldine, Kyla and Beto and his team of movers were all very personable and professional. They maintained excellent communication with us throughout our moving process. They made a very stressful situation pleasant and enjoyable. We felt so comfortable with Geraldine when she visited our home for the inventory and offered suggestions and encouragement for preparing to move. Kyla was in frequent communication and so patient with editing my changing inventory and arranging a schedule for pick up. Beto and his team of movers were friendly while remaining focused on their individual tasks and their teamwork was remarkable. They wrapped each piece of furniture so well and

results.title: Cincinnati’s 2026 Juneteenth Guide - Movers & Makers
results.content:
Movers & Makers



Sponsored Content

# Cincinnati’s 2026 Juneteenth Guide

by Movers and Makers •

## Join the National Underground Railroad Center on June 19

The National Underground Railroad Freedom Center will transform Freedom Way into a jubilant celebration of true independence on Friday, June 19, 2026, through a dynamic lineup of programming that brings the community together. [...] ## Opal’s Walk for Freedom

New this year to start the day’s festivities is the 2026 National Opal’s Walk for Freedom, which kicks off at 10 a.m. in front of the Freedom Center. Participants walk 2.5 miles, symbolizing the 2.5 years between the Emancipation Proclamation in 1863 and June 1865, when Union troops arrived in Galveston, Texas, to issue General Orders No. 3, which freed more than 250,000 enslaved people.

Organized by Texas-based non-profit Unity Unlimited, Inc., the walk is inspired by Dr. Opal Lee, the “Grandmother of Juneteenth,” who, at age 89, walked more than 1,400 miles from Fort Worth, Texas, to Washington, D.C. advocating for Juneteenth to be recognized as a federal holiday. [...] ### Performers and Special Guests

 2nd Wind Band
 Denise Driehaus, Hamilton County Commissioner
 Disney’s The Lion King, Broadway in Cincinnati and Cincinnati Arts Association
 DJ Arie
 DJ Vader
 Ijo Ugo Performing Arts LLC
 Kyle Inskeep, Local 12 anchor
 Orlando Brown, Jr., Bengals offensive tackle
 Skate Downtown Cincy

results.title: Top 13 Agentic AI Trends to Watch in 2026 - Firecrawl
results.content:
Read more: Why CLIs are better for AI coding agents than IDEs · Why AI agents prefer the CLI: token math and Terminal-Bench data · Claude Code vs OpenCode: which terminal AI coding agent should you use?

### 2. MCP went from hate to love — and the data backs it up

In early 2026, MCP felt like a punchline. Developer threads on X and Hacker News were full of criticism: setup is painful, the token overhead is enormous, and why would you burn 32,000–82,000 tokens on an MCP operation when a direct CLI call costs ~200? As trend #1 above shows, that criticism holds in the right context. CLI wins for token-efficient production pipelines.

But mid-2026 looks very different. Google Trends shows a clear resurgence in MCP search interest through the first half of 2026: [...] Beyond the EU, we're seeing movement in the U.S. with executive orders on AI safety, China's regulations on generative AI, and industry-specific frameworks in healthcare and finance.

### 6. Personal AI assistants are going mainstream

Personal AI agents are moving from tech demos to daily tools. The shift accelerated dramatically with clawd.bot's viral moment in January 2026, showing what happens when an AI assistant is given the right permissions and tools to act on your behalf. See our OpenClaw + Firecrawl guide for a hands-on walkthrough of building and deploying your own, or browse the best OpenClaw skills to extend what your agent can do out of the box. To pick the right search backend for your OpenClaw agent, see our comparison of the best OpenClaw search providers. [...] Spotify built an internal tool called "Honk" that lets engineers deploy features in minutes by describing what they want in plain English through Slack. The system uses generative AI, including Claude Code, to handle remote code deployment in real time. The result? Their best developers haven't written a line of code since December-they're orchestrating AI agents instead.

(P.S: We built a similar tool called Spring at Firecrawl. Spring is our 'super agent' and is used across engineering, marketing, sales, support, and admin - from within Slack!).

results.title: How Many Satellites Are in Orbit in 2026? — Live Count | Orbital Radar
results.content:
The pace of launches continues to accelerate. SpaceX alone is launching 20–60 Starlink satellites every 2–3 weeks, and Amazon's Project Kuiper is rapidly scaling deployment toward its 3,236-satellite target. China's Qianfan constellation, with up to 14,000 satellites planned, could further accelerate the trend. Projections suggest the total number of active satellites may reach 30,000–60,000 by 2030.

Explore the full orbital population on Orbital Radar's interactive 3D globe — including live positions, orbits, and object details.

## Why Tracking Matters [...] ## Satellites by Operator

The satellite industry has become increasingly concentrated among a few mega-constellation operators. The five largest operators account for the majority of all active spacecraft. For the full ranking, see Satellites by Operator.

## Growth Over Time

The number of active satellites in orbit has grown at an extraordinary rate, particularly since 2019 when mega-constellation deployments began in earnest. More satellites were placed in orbit in the last three years than in the entire previous history of spaceflight — a trend driven almost entirely by SpaceX. See the full launch log for a detailed record. [...] ## Satellites by Orbit Type

Active satellites occupy four main orbital bands, each suited to different missions. The overwhelming majority now reside in Low Earth Orbit, driven by the rapid deployment of mega-constellations like Starlink.

LEO's dominance is a recent phenomenon. Before 2019, the distribution was more balanced, with GEO hosting the majority of commercial communications satellites. The shift to LEO reflects both the economics of mega-constellations (lower launch cost, lower latency for internet service) and advances in small satellite technology.

results.content:
Projects

Projects

DART is a critical connector for North Texas, improving mobility for everyone – even those who don’t ride because the more people who ride, the fewer vehicles on the road. As our region grows, so must DART. Learn about some of the key projects underway that will make DART a viable option for even more North Texans.

Procurement

Procurement

From ongoing operations to capital projects, DART is a major consumer of products and services and an economic engine in North Texas. Click here for information and resources about doing business with DART.

Budget

Budget

• Let Alexa help with shopping: Alexa can be a powerful shopping assistant during Prime Day. If you’ve saved items to your wish list, cart, or “Save for Later,” Alexa can notify you up to 24 hours in advance when those items go on sale. You can even ask Alexa to remind you or make the purchase for you by saying, “Alexa, buy it for me.”

Over four days, shoppers will have access to millions of deals across categories including tech, home, fashion, kitchen, beauty, and entertainment. results.title: How Digital Strategies Transform Pharma Supply Chains in 2026 | Pharmaceutical Commerce
results.content:
### Blockchain-Augmented Serialization

Blockchain remains less a standalone solution than an interoperability layer that complements serialization and DSCSA compliance. AI-augmented traceability hubs that combine blockchain’s tamper-evidence with AI-driven anomaly detection are increasingly viewed as the architecture of choice for cross-border verification, anti-counterfeit defense, and large-scale recall management.

### Sequencing Enablers [...] ### Cloud-based ERP and Quality Backbones

A modern cloud ERP—most commonly an S/4HANA-class system—remains the backbone for system of record functions covering procurement, planning, manufacturing, transportation, billing, and financial flows. Cloud-based laboratory information management systems (LIMS), quality management systems (QMS), and content management platforms convert manual, paper-based good-practice processes into auditable digital workflows, addressing both data integrity and DSCSA documentation requirements simultaneously. Cloud adoption also reduces capital intensity, accelerates global deployment, and enables continuous regulatory updates.

### Big Data, Generative AI, and Agentic AI [...] ### Q&A: Why Pharma Supply Chains Are Turning to AI
